title: arctous alpina subsp. erythrocarpa
intro: Arctous alpina subsp. erythrocarpa, also known as the red-fruited bearberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a low-growing, deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by red berries and leathery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as urinary tract infections, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties can be prepared through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can provide relief from various health ailments, including improved cardiovascular health and immune system function.
